title: Stuck at the beginning of my trading carreer
author: Ok_Reference_5808
selftext:
Hi, I've been into trading for about 2 years, and I've been into automated trading for around 8 months. I'm kinda stuck and don't know where to continue with my trading.

I have done 5 challenges with FTMO and passed 1 of them. This was because I got lucky. My stop-loss and trailing stop-loss (TSL) in my EA aren't always working. There, I decided to make another strategy which only used stop-loss and take profit. This was working great until one day the price dropped really low. According to my stop-loss, I was supposed to lose 80, but eventually, I lost 600, which caused me to lose my funded account.

What do you advise me to do? Because ChatGPT isn't really working, and I don't know how to code. Do I need to switch brokers (I now use MetaTrader 5)? Any tips are welcome. Thanks in advance!

comments.author: AIdiegodf
comments.body:
If your stop-loss isn’t being respected, that’s less an AI problem and more a market structure / execution issue. Slippage, gaps, and liquidity conditions can break assumptions that look fine in backtests.

Before changing brokers or strategies, I’d slow things down and isolate the problem: test with smaller size, avoid news-heavy periods, and focus on understanding exactly why the stop wasn’t filled as expected. Automation can amplify mistakes just as easily as it amplifies edges, especially without full control over execution logic.

comments.author: rt3d02
comments.body:
First, switch to a better platform, as it's well known that MetaTrader 4/5 is not the best choice you are 100% guaranteed to loose your capital. 

secondly, There is no such thing as a magic indicator, automated EA, or strategy that will win 80% of your trades.

If you want to succeed, I recommend switching to a platform like NinjaTrader 8, focus on building a trading strategy based on "price" and backtest the strategy across all market conditions. Backtesting is key to your success. personally, as a successful trader, I use BacktestPods .com to backtest all my strategies, it has tremendously helped me on my journey, and I still use it to build statistics and performance metrics over time.
